skills.prompt: This topic covers creating line plots with fractions for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(A)). Students should understand how to organize fractional data on a number line using X marks or dots to create a line plot. Key concepts: line plots display data along a number line; each data point is represented by a mark above the corresponding value; when data involves fractions, the number line must include fractional increments (halves, quarters, eighths); data should be sorted and counted before plotting. Students should choose appropriate scales with fractional values and accurately place data points. Vocabulary: line plot, data, fraction, number line, frequency, scale, increment, mark, value, half, quarter, eighth. Common errors include using incorrect scales, miscounting data points, or placing marks at wrong positions. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

This topic covers creating stem-and-leaf plots for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(A)). Students should understand how to organize numerical data into a stem-and-leaf plot that shows the shape and distribution of data. Key concepts: the stem represents the leading digit(s) (usually tens place) and the leaf represents the trailing digit (usually ones place); leaves must be ordered from least to greatest; a key explains how to read the values; stem-and-leaf plots preserve individual data values while showing the overall distribution. Students should practice organizing data sets into stem-and-leaf plots accurately. Vocabulary: stem, leaf, plot, data, key, digit, tens place, ones place, distribution, order, organize, leading digit. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Create frequency charts with numerical data
skills.prompt: This topic covers creating frequency charts for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(A)). Students should understand how to organize data into a frequency chart (frequency table) that shows how often each value or category occurs. Key concepts: frequency is the number of times a value appears in a data set; data is organized into categories or intervals; tally marks can be used to count occurrences; the frequency column shows the total count for each category. Students should accurately count and record data, choose appropriate categories or intervals, and verify that the total of all frequencies equals the number of data points. Vocabulary: frequency, chart, table, tally, category, interval, data, count, total, organize, value. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Create line plots with whole numbers
skills.prompt: This topic covers creating line plots with whole numbers for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(A)). Students should understand how to organize whole number data on a number line to create a line plot. Key concepts: a line plot displays the frequency of data values along a number line; each data point is marked with an X or dot above its value; the scale should cover all data values; line plots make it easy to see which values occur most and least frequently. Students should practice collecting data, organizing it, and creating accurate line plots. Vocabulary: line plot, data, number line, frequency, scale, X mark, value, range, most frequent, least frequent. Problems should involve real-world data sets appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

This topic covers interpreting line plots for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(B)). Students should understand how to read and analyze data displayed in line plots to answer questions and draw conclusions. Key concepts: identify the most and least common values (mode); find the range (difference between greatest and least values); determine the total number of data points; calculate the sum of all values using the plot; answer comparison questions (how many more/fewer). Students should use operations with the data values, including fractions when applicable, to solve problems. Vocabulary: line plot, data, frequency, mode, range, total, interpret, analyze, compare, most common, least common, sum, difference. Problems should involve drawing conclusions from line plots appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Create and interpret line plots with fractions
skills.prompt: This topic covers creating line plots with fractions for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(B)). Students should understand how to organize fractional data on a number line using X marks or dots to create a line plot. Key concepts: line plots display data along a number line; each data point is represented by a mark above the corresponding value; when data involves fractions, the number line must include fractional increments (halves, quarters, eighths); data should be sorted and counted before plotting. Students should choose appropriate scales with fractional values and accurately place data points. Vocabulary: line plot, data, fraction, number line, frequency, scale, increment, mark, value, half, quarter, eighth. Common errors include using incorrect scales, miscounting data points, or placing marks at wrong positions. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

This topic covers interpreting frequency charts for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(B)). Students should understand how to read and analyze data from frequency charts to answer questions and draw conclusions. Key concepts: reading frequencies for specific categories; comparing frequencies between categories; finding the total number of data points by adding all frequencies; identifying the most and least frequent categories; using frequency data to make comparisons and solve multi-step problems. Vocabulary: frequency, chart, table, category, interpret, compare, total, most frequent, least frequent, data, analyze, conclusion. Problems should involve answering questions from frequency charts with real-world contexts appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

This topic covers interpreting stem-and-leaf plots for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(B)). Students should understand how to read and analyze data from stem-and-leaf plots to answer questions. Key concepts: reading individual values by combining the stem and leaf; finding the minimum and maximum values; determining the range, median, and mode from the plot; counting the total number of data points; identifying clusters, gaps, and outliers in the distribution. Vocabulary: stem-and-leaf plot, stem, leaf, key, median, mode, range, minimum, maximum, cluster, gap, outlier, distribution, interpret, analyze. Students should practice extracting information and making comparisons from stem-and-leaf plots. Problems should be appropriate for 9-10 year olds.

skills.skill: Interpret frequency charts with numerical data
skills.prompt: This topic covers interpreting frequency charts for Grade 4 students (TEKS 4.9(B)). Students should understand how to read and analyze data from frequency charts to answer questions and draw conclusions. Key concepts: reading frequencies for specific categories; comparing frequencies between categories; finding the total number of data points by adding all frequencies; identifying the most and least frequent categories; using frequency data to make comparisons and solve multi-step problems. Vocabulary: frequency, chart, table, category, interpret, compare, total, most frequent, least frequent, data, analyze, conclusion. Problems should involve answering questions from frequency charts with real-world contexts appropriate for 9-10 year olds.
